name: Greek entity tethys
shortDescription: Tethys is a Titan goddess of the primal font of fresh water which nourished the world. She is the wife of Oceanus and mother of the river gods and Oceanids.
description: Tethys is a Titan goddess in Greek mythology, representing the primal font of fresh water that nourished the world. She is the daughter of Uranus (Heaven) and Gaia (Earth), and the wife of her brother, Oceanus, the Titan god of the great, earth-encircling river Oceanus. Together, Tethys and Oceanus are the parents of numerous offspring, including the river gods (Potamoi) and the Oceanids, who are nymphs of springs, rivers, and fountains. Tethys's role is primarily associated with the nurturing and sustaining aspects of water, representing the source of life and fertility. While she doesn't feature prominently in many myths, her significance lies in her primordial status and her role as the mother of the world's rivers and springs. During the Titanomachy, the great war between the Titans and the Olympian gods, Tethys, along with Oceanus, remained neutral, refusing to take sides. This neutrality allowed them to maintain their position after the Olympian gods triumphed. Tethys is also sometimes associated with the constellation Cetus (the Whale) due to her connection with the sea. She represents the fundamental life-giving properties of fresh water and is an important figure in the early cosmology of the Greeks.
